segments.title: Radix Hydration useId Mismatch
segments.summary: Peter initiates a discussion about a Radix hydration issue affecting Alpha 3. Justin explains that the root cause is a `useId` mismatch between SSR and the client, as the counter diverges. He prototyped a deterministic wrapper for `useId` as a temporary solution, as patching Radix is too brittle. They agree to ship this 'band-aid' fix in Alpha 4 and upgrade to React 19 later.
decisions: Ship the `useId` wrapper as a temporary fix in Alpha 4.
segments.retrieval_summary: Decide to ship `useId` wrapper as temporary fix for radix hydration mismatch in `alpha_4` and postpone react_19 upgrade.

segments.title: Old vs. New Rendering Model Explanation
segments.summary: Peter requests an explanation of the old versus new rendering models. Justin clarifies that the old model was a single pass, while the new model isolates and streams the app and document renders separately before stitching them, which keeps `useId` counters aligned. Peter then asks to log the specific Radix components causing issues.
actions: Justin: Write up the flow of the old vs. new rendering model.
Justin: Log the exact Radix components that are throwing errors.
segments.retrieval_summary: Explain new rendering model that isolates and streams app/document renders to align `useId` counters; Justin to document and log problematic radix components.

segments.title: Action Plan for Radix Hydration Fix
segments.summary: Justin identifies the problematic Radix components as Dialog, Popover, and some form controls, noting that reproductions are reliable on hard reloads. Peter outlines the shipping plan: create documentation, apply the patch, write tests, and keep the wrapper behind a feature flag if necessary. Justin agrees and starts working on a branch and release notes.
actions: Justin: List the specific Radix components that throw errors.
Justin: Push a branch with the fix.
Justin: Draft notes for the fix.
decisions: The shipping plan will be: document, patch, test, and use a feature flag for the wrapper if needed.
segments.retrieval_summary: Plan to fix radix hydration issue by documenting, patching, testing, and using a feature flag for the `useId` wrapper.
